context: Lymph Node Features Suspicious For Malignancy √  size increase of > 100% over baseline: √  size > 3.3 cm ◊  Size of node usually NOT useful criterion (although small nodes have high NPV) √  change in shape: √round shape / long-to-short axis ratio of < 2 √  focal bulge / eccentric cortical thickening √  spiculation of margins √  intranodal microcalcifications (without history of gold Rx) √  loss of radiolucent fatty center / hilar notch √  increase in density Ultrasound of Malignant Lymph Nodes Ultrasound is the primary nonsurgical method for evaluating axillary nodes! Value:   62-92% sensitive
